title: Problema Coaliției este chiar ”COALIȚIA”
summary: Former coalition participant Ionuț Moșteanu argues that the institutionalization of weekly coalition meetings undermines effective governance. He contends that once a prime minister and cabinet receive parliamentary investiture, party leaders should not micromanage executive decisions, and cites the 2021 Anghel Saligny program under Florin Cîțu as an example of poor coalition-driven fiscal decisions.
talking_points: Weekly coalition leadership meetings create a para-constitutional decision-making body that lacks executive mandate
Once a government receives parliamentary investiture, party leaders should let ministers govern according to the agreed program
The coalition as institution should only intervene for major directional changes, not routine governance
The 2021 Anghel Saligny local investment program (10 billion euros) was a fiscally irresponsible coalition decision that undermined EU recovery fund implementation
Government performance should be measured by implementation of the governance program, with censure motions as the accountability mechanism
author: Ionuț Moşteanu
